title: `podman machine list` returns wrong units for Memory and Disk size
body:
### Issue Description

In Podman v5, `podman machine list` returns wrong units for Memory and DiskSize.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```
% git clone https://github.com/containers/podman.git
% cd podman
% make podman-remote
% ./bin/darwin/podman machine init
% ./bin/darwin/podman machine list
NAME                    VM TYPE     CREATED         LAST UP     CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default  applehv     11 minutes ago  Never       5           2KiB        100B
```

### Describe the results you received

```
MEMORY      DISK SIZE
2KiB        100B
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
MEMORY      DISK SIZE
2GiB        100GiB
```

### podman info output

### Upstream Latest Release

comments.body:
In Podman Desktop, when the podman machine is stopped, it returns incorrect units as well. (No problem when running).
![Screenshot 2024-03-03 at 1 09 04](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/54387703/1c1e56a6-f2ff-4973-aa03-d88ba51d8ab1)

On a side note, it would be nice if Podman Desktop would switch to use the _same_ units

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Byte#Multiple-byte_units - currently "2.15 GB, 107.37 GB"
